response.text:
# REFLECTION

We are in a severe grid-wide energy crisis with mutual rejection deadlock. Our SoC of 0.30 kWh (10% of 3 kWh capacity) is critically low and below all peers' sharing thresholds; r3c1 and r3c3 have repeatedly rejected our requests citing their own survival constraints (60% and 40% minimums respectively). We are islanded with a 1.00 kW load, giving us approximately 18 minutes of autonomy before battery depletion—far below the ~1.8 hour estimate in our belief state, suggesting load or SoC measurement drift. The network shows no viable inbound supply: r3c1 offers only ~0.04 kW (insufficient), r3c3 is at 11% SoC (critical), and r4c2 is requesting energy from us despite being below threshold themselves. Outbound sharing is impossible without risking immediate blackout. Our only viable paths are immediate load reduction (to extend runway and signal desperation to peers), aggressive inbound requests to any peer showing recovery, or grid reconnection. We must abandon the assumption that r3c1 is reliable and shift to pure survival mode.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.15
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: [r3c1, r3c3, r4c2]
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Grid-wide scarcity deadlock; all peers below or at survival thresholds. SoC 0.30/3 kWh (10%) with 1.00 kW load = ~18 min autonomy. No viable inbound supply. Immediate load reduction and grid reconnection are only viable survival paths. Suspend all outbound sharing; accept only inbound offers from peers above 70% SoC."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
